> Updated each CI run from the same data files that drive the site.

Sitemap: https://playersb.com/sitemap.xml
Per-team feeds: https://playersb.com/teams/<slug>/feed.xml
ai.txt: https://playersb.com/ai.txt

## Learn topics
- Per-90 normalization → https://playersb.com/learn/per-90-normalization/
  Per-90 rates let you compare output when players have different minutes, roles, or match availability.
- Efficiency vs volume → https://playersb.com/learn/efficiency-vs-volume/
  Great output can come from high volume, elite efficiency, or both. Learn to separate them.
- Reading a comparison → https://playersb.com/learn/reading-comparisons/
  Use comparison outputs as a learning brief: rate, efficiency, and role context in one view.
- Minutes and role context → https://playersb.com/learn/minutes-and-role/
  Minutes and role determine opportunity. Context tells you if a rate is sustainable.
- Building comparison briefs → https://playersb.com/learn/building-comparison-briefs/
  Use a structured brief to summarize a comparison for scouting notes or team discussions.
- Expected goals (xG), explained → https://playersb.com/learn/expected-goals-explained/
  xG is a probability score for each shot. Treat it as chance quality, not a prediction.
- Decoding position labels → https://playersb.com/learn/position-taxonomy/
  Position codes are coarse — the actual role depends on the team’s shape, the manager, and the player’s tendencies.
- Reading defensive metrics → https://playersb.com/learn/defensive-metrics/
  Defensive counts are noisy because they depend on opportunity. Always normalise by opponent possession and team shape.
- Set-piece contribution → https://playersb.com/learn/set-piece-contribution/
  Set pieces are a stable share of goals and assists — isolating them sharpens every comparison.
- Form vs trend → https://playersb.com/learn/form-vs-trend/
  Five-match form is mostly noise. Trends across 20–30 matches reveal real change.

## Glossary
- Per-90: A rate statistic that scales output to a 90-minute match so players with different minutes can be compared fairly.
- Minutes: Total playing time. High totals can reflect durability or role; always pair with per-90 rates for context.
- Goals per shot: An efficiency indicator showing how often a shot becomes a goal. Useful alongside shot volume.
- Shot accuracy: Percentage of shots that are on target. A quick proxy for finishing quality and decision making.
- Shots per 90: Shot volume normalized to a full match. Highlights how often a player gets attempts.
- Touches in box: How often a player gets touches in the penalty area. Often correlated with goal involvement.
- xG (Expected Goals): An estimate of scoring probability based on shot quality, location, and context. Used to separate chance quality from finishing.
- xA (Expected Assists): An estimate of the likelihood a pass becomes an assist based on chance quality. Useful for evaluating creative output.
- Role: The tactical job a player performs (e.g., target striker, pressing winger). Role changes how stats should be interpreted.
- Volume vs efficiency: Volume reflects how often actions occur (shots/90), while efficiency reflects how well they convert (goals per shot).
